name: Jimmy McHugh
abstract: James Francis McHugh (July 10, 1894 – May 23, 1969) was an American composer. One of the most prolific songwriters from the 1920s to the 1950s, he is credited with over 500 songs.

works_created.t: I just found out about love

about_quotes.speaker: Laurence Juber
about_quotes.evidence: My friend Lee, Lee Newman, is the grandson of Jimmy McHugh, the songwriter. And so I’ve thrown in, on my last “But One” album I did “I Can’t Give You Anything But Love, Baby”... which are all Jimmy McHugh songs.
about_quotes.doc_title: Laurence Juber interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 21, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Greer
about_quotes.evidence: We had special writers, Harold Arlen, Jimmy McHugh and Dorothy Fields. They were writers, they were on the staff

about_quotes.speaker: Lizzie Miles
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy McHugh of the Mills staff, music staff, taking me to the Victor and played my first test on the Victor
